nodes.title: Rise of Right Wing
nodes.description: Growing far-right movements globally, fueled by unhappiness, misinformation, and scapegoating of immigrants.

nodes.title: Lobbying
nodes.description:
Companies in charge of policies — fundamental conflict of interest.
Fake research able to impact policies.
Green washing: fake Carbon Neutral initiatives (1 step forward, 2 steps backward).

nodes.title: Cost of Living
nodes.description:
Rising costs across food, accommodation, and utilities.
Misinformation distorts public understanding of the real causes.

nodes.title: Climate Change
nodes.description:
Driven by CO2, methane, pollution.
Denied through misinformation and fake research.
Includes climate denialism and fake research.

nodes.title: Over Exploitation
nodes.description: Overfishing, deforestation (forest burning to make space for farming), mining, steel, water table / aquifer depletion.

nodes.title: Pollution
nodes.description:
Causes illness and cancer.
Microplastics, pesticides (killing bees, causing cancer), air pollution (nano particles).

Misinformation: fake research, consumer blaming ("what pollutes the most?"), buying new "green" cars pollutes as well.

nodes.title: Illness
nodes.description: COVID sequel — experts say it is only a matter of time.

nodes.title: Food Supply
nodes.description:
Threatened by climate change.
Need for new crops.
Caused by climate change disrupting agriculture.

nodes.title: Antibiotic Resistance
nodes.description: Growing resistance threatening the effectiveness of medical treatments worldwide.

nodes.title: Unhappiness
nodes.description: General population discontent driving political extremism.

nodes.title: Immigration Scapegoating
nodes.description:
Immigration used as easy blame target by right-wing movements.
Matches patterns in Eco's definition of fascism.

nodes.title: Eco's Fascism Definition
nodes.description:
Umberto Eco's features of fascism:
• Cult of tradition
• Rejection of modernism
• Disagreement is treason
• Fear of difference
• Appeal to social frustration
• The obsession of a plot
• Humiliation by the wealth/force of others
• Pacifism is trafficking with the enemy
• Contempt for the weak

nodes.title: Corporate Policy Control
nodes.description:
Companies directly influencing and controlling policy decisions.
Fundamental conflict of interest between profit and public good.

nodes.title: Green Washing
nodes.description:
Fake Carbon Neutral initiatives.
1 step forward, 2 steps backward.
Creates illusion of progress while undermining real climate action.

nodes.title: Accommodation Crisis
nodes.description:
Causes:
• Short term rentals (Airbnb)
• Company ownership of housing
• Foreign investment

High demand: immigration, tourism, investment (low interest → housing speculation), globalization (anyone can buy anywhere).

Low supply: slow to build, expensive space, lack of workforce, high construction cost (concrete is energy intensive), lots of building regulations, lack of space, no need to sell early.

Solutions:
• Countryside?
• High density
• We already have enough housing — how many are empty?
• 30% of office space is empty → transform to housing? (very expensive)
• Subventions with conditions

nodes.title: Utilities Crisis
nodes.description:
Water: over-use and waste.
Electricity: energy crisis from Ukraine-Russia war.

nodes.title: Extreme Weather
nodes.description:
Thunder, floods, drought, forest fires.
Increasing frequency and intensity.

nodes.title: Ocean Acidification
nodes.description:
Ocean is the #1 reserve of CO2.
More CO2 → more acid → coral reef death.
Long-term cascading effects on marine ecosystems.

nodes.title: Fast Fashion
nodes.description:
Textile waste contributing to pollution and resource depletion.
Massive environmental footprint from production and disposal.

nodes.title: Transportation
nodes.description:
Need to favor local production and consumption.
Supply chain tracking and tracing needed for accountability.

nodes.title: Outdated Growth Model
nodes.description:
Budgets are Ponzi Schemes — require endless growth.
Declining birth rate undermining the model.
Relies on mass immigration to keep growing.

How long will countries let their citizens leave?

nodes.title: Loneliness Epidemic
nodes.description:
Growing isolation manifesting as:
• Pet hoarding
• Incel culture
• Addictions

nodes.title: Underfunded Education System
nodes.description:
Chronically low budget.
Unable to adapt to modern needs and challenges.

nodes.title: Under funded Health System
nodes.description:
Low budget.
Struggling with growing elderly population.

nodes.title: Obesity
nodes.description: Driven by food quality and lack of dietary diversity.

nodes.title: Mass Immigration
nodes.description:
Challenges of inclusion and integration at scale.
Driven by declining birth rate in developed countries.

nodes.title: Artificial Intelligence
nodes.description:
Uncertain societal impact.
Potential for both disruption and solutions.

nodes.title: Bias & Discrimination
nodes.description: Systemic bias and discrimination embedded in institutions and society.

nodes.title: Declining Birth Rate
nodes.description:
Already happening in developed countries.
Countries rely on immigration to avoid economic breakdown.
Inadequate policies + mass immigration creates its own set of issues.

While world population might start to decline in 2075, it is already happening in developed countries.

See: https://population.un.org/wpp/Graphs/DemographicProfiles/Line/900

nodes.title: Misinformation
nodes.description:
Seems to be the crux of our society right now. Everything is impacted.

Key questions:
• How to find the right information?
• How to reach the person needing the information?
• How to build trust with people so they can trust the information?
• How to spark constructive discussions?

Effects:
• Causes people to mistrust everything — cannot distinguish truth from lie
• Too much information
• Fake science — cannot make our own research, need a trusted baseline
• Science is unsure by nature (correlation ≠ causation)
• Peer review is failing (bias, conflict of interest, funding sources)
• Propaganda on social networks (Cambridge Analytica)

Finding previously seen information is IMPOSSIBLE:
• Even Arte YouTube channel unlists documentaries after some time
• Internet Archive + Wayback Machine: https://web.archive.org/

Wikipedia has information but:
• Not structured for this purpose
• Not a place for discussions or opinions
• Need to mix facts with opinions while avoiding bot farms

nodes.title: Fake Research
nodes.description:
Known examples:
• BPA
• Bees / insects
• Cigarettes
• Teflon / PFOA / PFAS / PFOS

See: "La Fabrique de l'Ignorance" (Arte)
https://campus.arte.tv/program/la-fabrique-de-l-ignorance

nodes.title: Population Dynamics
nodes.description:
World population may start declining around 2075.
Already happening in developed countries which started relying on immigration to avoid economic breakdown.
Inadequate policies + mass immigration started their own set of issues.

See: https://population.un.org/wpp/Graphs/DemographicProfiles/Line/900

nodes.title: Climate Denialism
nodes.description:
Active denial of climate science.
Fueled by misinformation and fake research funded by fossil fuel lobbying.

nodes.title: Pesticides
nodes.description:
Killing bees and other pollinators.
Linked to cancer in humans.
Fake research used to downplay risks.

nodes.title: Microplastics
nodes.description:
Pervasive contamination of water, food, and air.
Health effects still being uncovered.

nodes.title: Deforestation
nodes.description:
Forest burning to make space for farming.
Destroying carbon sinks and biodiversity.

nodes.title: Overfishing
nodes.description:
Depleting fish stocks faster than they can recover.
Threatening ocean ecosystems and food security.

nodes.title: Cost of Living - Why?
nodes.description:
Why did the price increase?

High demand:
• Immigration
• Tourism (Airbnb)
• Investment instead of housing (low interest, easy loans, no interest in saving in bank)
• Housing is speculation now
• Globalization — anyone can buy anywhere

Low supply:
• Slow to build (space is expensive, lack of workforce, high construction cost, concrete is energy intensive, lots of rules)
• Return on investment is low → less construction
• Lack of space — wait for price increase, no need to sell early

Propriété foncière: divide resources so they are split between people.

nodes.title: Inequalities (Wealth)
nodes.description:
Wealth imbalance (super rich) creates a shift in power.

The level of wealth is too high, and it can be used to sway the outdated democratic system still in place

nodes.title: Artists get a fraction of the revenues
Social networks
nodes.description:
Social networks use rage bait, misinformation to drive engagement.
Anger is the biggest driver of engagement

nodes.title: Service Economy
nodes.description:
* Creation of more and more bullshit jobs
* Job satisfaction is low
* People are looking for things that matters

nodes.title: AI Bias
nodes.description: I and automated decision-making embedding and amplifying existing discrimination in hiring, lending, policing, healthcare. Links to: Bias & Discrimination, Artificial Intelligence

nodes.title: E-Waste
nodes.description: Growing mountain of electronic waste, often exported to developing countries. Toxic materials leaching into soil and water

nodes.title: Digital Divide
nodes.description: Unequal access to technology and internet, especially in rural and low-income areas. Widens inequality and limits education access.

nodes.title: Surveillance Capitalism
nodes.description: Personal data as the primary business model of the internet. Drives enshittification, social network toxicity, and erodes privacy. Links to: Enshittification, Social Networks, Democracy Crisis

nodes.title: Sexist Medicine
nodes.description: New meds are often only tested on males because of the fluctuating female hormones that are hard to account for

nodes.title: Planned Obsolescence
nodes.description: Newer products are more complicated which might make them more convenient but also make them more likely to break

nodes.title: Regulatory Capture
nodes.description: Regulators becoming aligned with the industries they're supposed to oversee

nodes.title: Tax Evasion and Creative Tax practices
Erosion of Labor Rights
nodes.description: Gig economy, union busting, declining worker protections.

nodes.title: Intellectual Property Overreach
nodes.description: Patents on seeds (Monsanto), medicines, genes. Restricts access and innovation.

nodes.title: Monoculture Farming
nodes.description: Vast fields of single crops destroying soil health, requiring ever more fertilizer and pesticides. One disease can wipe out entire harvests (see: banana Cavendish crisis).

nodes.title: Seed Monopolies
nodes.description: A handful of companies (Bayer/Monsanto, Corteva, Syngenta) control the majority of commercial seeds. Farmers can't save seeds, traditional varieties disappear.

nodes.title: Food Waste
nodes.description: Roughly a third of all food produced globally is wasted. Cosmetic standards reject perfectly edible produce. Supermarket overstock models guarantee waste.

nodes.title: Soil Salinization
nodes.description: Irrigation in arid regions concentrating salt in topsoil, permanently destroying farmland. Massive problem in California, Australia, Pakistan.

nodes.title: Fertilizer Dependency
nodes.description: Industrial agriculture hooked on synthetic nitrogen fertilizer, which requires natural gas to produce. Price spikes (Ukraine war) immediately become food crises.

nodes.title: Pollinator Collapse
nodes.description: Beyond bees — butterflies, bats, hoverflies all declining. 75% of food crops depend on animal pollination.

nodes.title: Land Grabbing
nodes.description: Foreign governments and corporations buying up farmland in Africa and Southeast Asia, displacing local farmers. Food grown for export while locals go hungry.

nodes.title: Aquaculture Pollution
nodes.description: Fish farming concentrates waste, disease, antibiotics, and parasites. Sea lice devastating wild salmon populations near farms.

nodes.title: Glyphosate Everywhere
nodes.description: Found in rain, breast milk, cereal. Monsanto papers revealed suppression of cancer links. Regulatory agencies relied on industry-funded studies.

nodes.title: Nitrogen Runoff
nodes.description: Agricultural fertilizer washing into waterways, creating ocean dead zones (Gulf of Mexico, Baltic Sea). Algal blooms killing marine life.

nodes.title: Drug Patent Evergreening
nodes.description: Pharma companies making minor tweaks to extend patents and block generics. Insulin was discovered in 1921 and still costs hundreds of dollars in the US.

nodes.title: Clinical Trial Bias
nodes.description: Trials historically excluded women, elderly, and minorities. Dosages and side effects calibrated for young white men.

nodes.title: Orphan Disease Neglect
nodes.description: Rare diseases and tropical diseases affecting the poor get almost no R&D investment because the market is too small.

nodes.title: Pharmacy Benefit Managers
nodes.description: Middlemen extracting value from the drug supply chain, inflating costs while adding no health value. Opaque pricing.

nodes.title: Medical Device Regulation Gaps
nodes.description: 510(k) loophole in the US lets devices enter market by claiming similarity to existing ones, without new clinical trials. Leads to harm (metal hip implants, surgical mesh).

nodes.title: Healthcare Worker Burnout
nodes.description: Post-COVID exodus from nursing and medicine. Those who stay are overworked, underpaid, and traumatized. Systems running on fumes.

nodes.title: Sackler/Opioid Playbook
nodes.description: The pharmaceutical industry marketing addictive drugs as safe, buying doctors, suppressing evidence. A template that keeps being repeated.

nodes.title: Concrete Carbon Footprint
nodes.description: Cement production alone accounts for roughly 8% of global CO2 emissions. No scalable green alternative yet.

nodes.title: Skilled Labor Shortage
nodes.description: Trades stigmatized for decades, entire generation pushed toward university. Now there aren't enough electricians, plumbers, welders.

nodes.title: Building Code Ossification
nodes.description: Regulations written for 20th century construction methods. Innovative approaches (mass timber, modular, 3D printed) face years of bureaucratic resistance.

nodes.title: Asbestos Legacy
nodes.description: Millions of buildings worldwide still contain asbestos. Removal is expensive and dangerous. Developing countries still mining and using it.

nodes.title: Real Estate Money Laundering
nodes.description: Luxury property used globally to park illicit wealth. Entire neighborhoods of empty investment units while people can't find housing.

nodes.title: Infrastructure Decay
nodes.description: Bridges, water mains, electrical grids built mid-20th century approaching end of life simultaneously. The bill is trillions and nobody wants to pay it.

nodes.title: Construction Waste
nodes.description: Construction and demolition produce roughly 30-40% of total waste in many countries. Low recycling rates.

nodes.title: Shadow Banking
nodes.description: Massive unregulated financial activity outside traditional banks. Hedge funds, private credit, crypto — systemic risk with no oversight.

nodes.title: Algorithmic Trading Instability
nodes.description: High-frequency trading creating flash crashes and disconnecting markets from fundamentals. Machines trading with machines.

nodes.title: Predatory Lending
nodes.description: Payday loans, buy-now-pay-later targeting vulnerable populations. Debt spirals by design.

nodes.title: Credit Rating Oligopoly
nodes.description: Three companies (Moody's, S&P, Fitch) rate the entire world's debt. Massive conflicts of interest — they're paid by the entities they rate. Directly contributed to 2008 crash.

nodes.title: Pension Time Bomb
nodes.description: Defined benefit pensions underfunded everywhere. Public sector pension obligations that can't be met. The math doesn't work with aging populations.

nodes.title: Too Big to Fail (Still)
nodes.description: Banks are even bigger and more concentrated than before 2008. Dodd-Frank partially rolled back. Next crisis bails out the same people.

nodes.title: Cryptocurrency Energy Waste
nodes.description: Bitcoin alone consuming more electricity than many countries. Proof-of-work as a concept is environmentally indefensible.

nodes.title: Private Equity Gutting
nodes.description: PE firms buying companies, loading them with debt, extracting fees, then letting them collapse. Hospitals, newspapers, retail chains hollowed out.

nodes.title: Stranded Assets
nodes.description: Fossil fuel companies have reserves on their books worth trillions that can never be burned if we meet climate targets. They'll fight to burn them.

nodes.title: Grid Modernization Lag
nodes.description: Electrical grids designed for centralized power plants, now needing to handle distributed solar, wind, storage. Billions in upgrades needed.

nodes.title: Nuclear Stigma vs Reality
nodes.description: Nuclear is the safest energy source per kilowatt-hour statistically, but public fear (Chernobyl, Fukushima) blocks expansion. Meanwhile coal kills millions annually.

nodes.title: Rare Earth Dependency
nodes.description: Wind turbines, EV motors, batteries all need materials dominated by Chinese processing. Single point of geopolitical failure.

nodes.title: Methane Leaks
nodes.description: Natural gas marketed as clean bridge fuel, but methane leaks throughout the supply chain may make it worse than coal for climate in the short term. Industry underreports by orders of magnitude.

nodes.title: Energy Poverty
nodes.description: Hundreds of millions still cooking with wood and dung. Indoor air pollution killing millions. Clean energy transition bypasses the poorest.

nodes.title: Hydrogen Hype
nodes.description: Green hydrogen pushed as a solution for everything, but it's inefficient, expensive, and mostly produced from natural gas today (grey hydrogen marketed as clean).

nodes.title: Mining Community Destruction
nodes.description: Whether it's coal in Appalachia or lithium in Chile, extraction destroys local environments and communities, then companies leave when the resource runs out.

nodes.title: Technical Debt
nodes.description: Legacy systems running critical infrastructure (banking, government, healthcare) on decades-old code nobody fully understands. COBOL still runs much of global finance.

nodes.title: Open Source Sustainability
nodes.description: Critical internet infrastructure maintained by unpaid volunteers. One burned-out maintainer can compromise millions of systems (see: xz utils, Log4j).

nodes.title: Dark Patterns
nodes.description: UI designed to manipulate users into subscriptions, data sharing, purchases they didn't intend. Increasingly sophisticated and hard to regulate.

nodes.title: Vendor Lock-in
nodes.description: Cloud providers, enterprise software making it deliberately difficult and expensive to switch. Organizations trapped in escalating costs.

nodes.title: AI Training Data Exploitation
nodes.description: Models trained on artists' work, writers' content, coders' repositories without consent or compensation. Entire creative industries undermined by their own output.

nodes.title: Deepfakes
nodes.description: Synthetic media making it impossible to trust video or audio evidence. Already weaponized for political manipulation and non-consensual pornography.

nodes.title: Internet Centralization
nodes.description: A few companies (Cloudflare, AWS, Google) mediate most internet traffic. Single points of failure and control for the entire web.

nodes.title: Adtech Fraud
nodes.description: Roughly 30-40% of digital ad spending is fraud — bots clicking ads, fake websites, inflated metrics. Entire ecosystem built on lies. Nobody wants to audit it because everyone profits.

nodes.title: Microfiber Shedding
nodes.description: Every wash of synthetic clothing releases thousands of plastic microfibers into waterways. Filters don't catch them. One of the largest sources of ocean microplastics.

nodes.title: Textile Worker Exploitation
nodes.description: Rana Plaza collapsed in 2013, killing over 1,100 garment workers. Conditions have barely improved. Brands audit their own supply chains.

nodes.title: Fashion Returns Waste
nodes.description: Online fashion has 30-40% return rates. Most returned clothing is landfilled or incinerated because it's cheaper than reprocessing.

nodes.title: Cotton Water Footprint
nodes.description: A single cotton t-shirt takes roughly 2,700 liters of water to produce. The Aral Sea was destroyed for cotton farming.

nodes.title: Local News Desert
nodes.description: Local newspapers dying everywhere. When local journalism disappears, corruption increases, civic engagement drops, and misinformation fills the void.

nodes.title: Engagement-Driven Editorial
nodes.description: Even legacy media now writing headlines for clicks rather than accuracy. Nuance is algorithmically punished.

nodes.title: Media Ownership Consolidation
nodes.description: Fewer and fewer companies owning more outlets. Sinclair Broadcasting making hundreds of local stations read the same script.

nodes.title: Journalist Safety Crisis
nodes.description: Record numbers of journalists killed, imprisoned, and surveilled globally. Pegasus spyware used by governments against reporters.

nodes.title: Paywall Paradox
nodes.description: Quality journalism going behind paywalls means accurate information becomes a luxury good while misinformation is free and abundant.

nodes.title: Student Debt Anchor
nodes.description: Entire generation delaying homeownership, family formation, entrepreneurship because of education debt.

nodes.title: Credential Inflation
nodes.description: Jobs that previously required high school now demand degrees. Master's is the new bachelor's. Cost rises, value diminishes.

nodes.title: Teaching Profession Collapse
nodes.description: Low pay, high stress, political targeting. Teacher shortages becoming critical across developed countries.

nodes.title: Textbook Racket
nodes.description: Academic publishers charging hundreds of dollars for textbooks with trivial annual updates to kill the used market. Publicly funded research locked behind paywalls.

nodes.title: Shipping Emissions
nodes.description: Global shipping produces more CO2 than most countries. Runs on bunker fuel, the dirtiest petroleum product. Barely regulated.

nodes.title: Tire Microplastics
nodes.description: Tire wear is one of the largest sources of microplastic pollution. EVs are actually worse because they're heavier. Almost completely ignored in policy.

nodes.title: Aviation Climate Problem
nodes.description: No viable electric or hydrogen solution for long-haul flight. Sustainable aviation fuel exists but at a tiny fraction of needed scale. Carbon offsets for flights are largely theater.

nodes.title: Car-Dependent Urban Design
nodes.description: Decades of zoning for cars creating sprawl, isolation, obesity, pollution. Redesigning cities takes generations.

nodes.title: Last-Mile Delivery Explosion
nodes.description: Same-day and next-day delivery putting thousands more vans on roads, increasing congestion and emissions. Consumers rarely see the cost.

nodes.title: Lithium Battery Fire Risk
nodes.description: EV and e-bike battery fires increasingly common. Firefighters not equipped or trained. Recycling infrastructure barely exists.

nodes.title: Cobalt Child Labor
nodes.description: Significant portion of global cobalt (essential for batteries) comes from artisanal mines in DRC using child labor. Supply chain audits are performative.

nodes.title: Tailings Dam Failures
nodes.description: Mine waste dams collapsing with catastrophic consequences (Brumadinho, Mount Polley). Hundreds of at-risk dams worldwide. Companies self-report safety.

nodes.title: Deep Sea Mining
nodes.description: Push to mine the ocean floor for minerals before we even understand those ecosystems. Potentially irreversible damage.

nodes.title: Acid Mine Drainage
nodes.description: Abandoned mines leaching toxic metals into waterways for decades or centuries. Cleanup costs dumped on taxpayers.

nodes.title: Climate Uninsurability
nodes.description: Insurers withdrawing from wildfire zones, flood plains, hurricane corridors. When you can't get insurance, you can't get a mortgage. Entire regions becoming financially unviable.

nodes.title: Health Insurance Complexity as Feature
nodes.description: System deliberately complex so people don't use benefits they're entitled to. Prior authorization killing patients.

nodes.title: Actuarial Data Discrimination
nodes.description: Insurance pricing algorithms encoding racial and economic discrimination in ways that are technically legal.

nodes.title: Pharmaceutical Water Contamination
nodes.description: Antidepressants, hormones, antibiotics passing through wastewater treatment into rivers and drinking water. Fish changing sex. Long-term human effects unknown.

nodes.title: PFAS / Forever Chemicals
nodes.description: Found in virtually all human blood. Don't break down. In drinking water, soil, food packaging. Cleanup costs estimated in the hundreds of billions.

nodes.title: Privatization of Water
nodes.description: Nestlé and others extracting water from public aquifers for bottled water at near-zero cost. Water as commodity vs human right.

nodes.title: Aging Water Infrastructure
nodes.description: Lead pipes still delivering water in many cities (Flint was not unique). Trillions needed in upgrades globally.

nodes.title: Satellite Light Pollution
nodes.description: Starlink and others putting tens of thousands of satellites in orbit, ruining ground-based astronomy. Scientific observation becoming impossible.

nodes.title: Kessler Syndrome
nodes.description: Enough junk in orbit that collisions create more debris, potentially making orbit unusable. One bad collision could trigger a chain reaction.

nodes.title: Geoengineering Without Governance
nodes.description: Solar radiation management, ocean iron fertilization — any country or billionaire could unilaterally alter global climate. No international framework exists.

nodes.title: Synthetic Biology Risks
nodes.description: Gene drives, engineered organisms, DIY bio labs. Potential benefits are enormous, but accidental or intentional release of engineered organisms could be catastrophic.

nodes.title: Sandwich Generation
nodes.description: People simultaneously caring for aging parents and young children with no institutional support. Mostly falls on women. Links to burnout, workforce dropout, financial strain.

nodes.title: Male Loneliness / Purpose Crisis
nodes.description: Young men dropping out of education, workforce, social life. Radicalization pipelines exploiting this void.

nodes.title: Disability Invisibility
nodes.description: Infrastructure, technology, workplaces designed without consideration for the roughly 15% of the global population with disabilities.

nodes.title: Indigenous Land Rights
nodes.description: Ongoing dispossession, broken treaties, resource extraction on indigenous territories worldwide. Systemic erasure continuing in modern form.

nodes.title: Elder Abuse
nodes.description: Rising with aging populations. Financial exploitation, neglect in care homes, isolation. Severely underreported.

nodes.title: Child Screen Addiction
nodes.description: Developing brains shaped by algorithms optimized for engagement. Attention spans, social skills, sleep quality all measurably declining.

nodes.title: Gambling Normalization
nodes.description: Sports betting apps, loot boxes in games, crypto speculation blurring the line. Targeting young men specifically. Addiction rates climbing.

nodes.title: Long COVID
nodes.description: Chronic post-viral condition affecting millions, still poorly understood and underfunded. Massive workforce and quality-of-life impact.

nodes.title: Healthcare as Profit Center
nodes.description: Drug pricing, insurance complexity, hospital consolidation. Health outcomes inversely correlated with profit motive.

nodes.title: Gig Economy / Precarious Work
nodes.description: Workers without benefits, stability, or protections. Platforms externalize all risk onto individuals.

nodes.title: Water Wars
nodes.description: Increasing geopolitical conflict over freshwater resources (Nile, Mekong, Colorado River).

nodes.title: Resource Nationalism
nodes.description: Countries restricting export of critical minerals (China rare earths, Indonesia nickel). Weaponizing supply chains.

nodes.title: Nuclear Proliferation Risk
nodes.description: More unstable geopolitics increasing risk of nuclear weapons spread and use.

nodes.title: Renoviction
nodes.description: Landlords evicting tenants under guise of renovations to relist at higher rents. Widespread in Montreal, Toronto, Vancouver. Legal loopholes make it easy, enforcement is weak.

nodes.title: REIT Housing Financialization
nodes.description: Real Estate Investment Trusts buying up rental buildings, raising rents to maximize shareholder returns. Akelius, Starlight, CAPREIT turning housing into financial products.

nodes.title: Temporary Foreign Worker Housing Abuse
nodes.description: TFW program workers crammed into employer-provided housing, paying inflated rents deducted from wages. Tied visas mean they can't complain.

nodes.title: Zoning as NIMBYism
nodes.description: Single-family zoning in Canadian cities blocking densification. Homeowners fighting multi-unit builds to protect property values. Montreal less affected than Toronto/Vancouver but growing.

nodes.title: Student Housing Crisis
nodes.description: International student enrollment massively expanded with zero matching housing investment. Students sleeping in shifts, living in illegal subdivisions.

nodes.title: Telecom Oligopoly (Rogers/Bell/Telus)
nodes.description: Three companies controlling Canadian wireless and internet. Among the highest prices in the developed world. CRTC regulatory capture. MVNOs squeezed out.

nodes.title: Rogers Outage 2022
nodes.description: Single point of failure took down 911, Interac payments, hospital systems nationwide for 19 hours. Demonstrated dangerous infrastructure concentration.

nodes.title: Rural Connectivity Gap
nodes.description: Millions of rural and northern Canadians without reliable broadband. Starlink filling gaps but at premium cost. First Nations communities worst served.

nodes.title: Grocery Oligopoly
nodes.description: Loblaws, Sobeys, Metro control ~60% of Canadian grocery. Bread price-fixing scandal. Shrinkflation rampant. Loblaw owns Shoppers Drug Mart, Joe Fresh, PC Financial — vertical integration.

nodes.title: Bread Price-Fixing Scandal
nodes.description: Loblaws, Weston, and others fixed bread prices for 14+ years. $25 gift card offered as settlement. No executives jailed. Class action ongoing.

nodes.title: Food Bank Usage Records
nodes.description: Food bank visits hitting record highs year after year in Canada. Working people now a significant portion of users. Not just unemployment — cost of living outpacing wages.

nodes.title: Supply Management Debate
nodes.description: Canada's dairy/poultry/egg quota system keeps prices high for consumers but protects farmers. Under constant attack in trade deals (CUSMA). Small farms can't afford quotas anyway.

nodes.title: ER Wait Times Crisis
nodes.description: Canadian ERs routinely 12-24+ hour waits. ERs closing temporarily in rural areas due to staff shortages. People dying in waiting rooms.

nodes.title: Family Doctor Shortage
nodes.description: 6.5+ million Canadians without a family doctor. Walk-in clinics overwhelmed. Foreign-trained doctors stuck in years of recertification. Doctors retiring faster than being replaced.

nodes.title: Creeping Healthcare Privatization
nodes.description: Provincial governments quietly expanding private clinics and services. Two-tier system emerging despite Canada Health Act. Ontario, Alberta, Quebec all moving this direction.

nodes.title: Pharmacare Still Missing
nodes.description: Canada is the only country with universal healthcare that doesn't include prescription drug coverage. 1 in 5 Canadians can't afford prescriptions.

nodes.title: Mental Health Access Gap
nodes.description: Therapy not covered by provincial health plans. Psychologists $200+/hr. Waitlists for publicly funded services 1-2 years. Crisis lines overwhelmed.

nodes.title: MAID Expansion Controversy
nodes.description: Medical Assistance in Dying expanded eligibility raising concerns that poverty, disability, and lack of social services are pushing people toward it as only option.

nodes.title: Credential Recognition Failure
nodes.description: Foreign-trained doctors, nurses, engineers driving taxis. Provincial licensing bodies acting as gatekeepers. Years of redundant training required. Massive waste of human capital.

nodes.title: Oil Sands Tailings Ponds
nodes.description: 1.4 trillion liters of toxic tailings in Alberta. Leaking into Athabasca River. No proven technology to remediate. Downstream First Nations communities reporting elevated cancer rates.

nodes.title: Orphan Wells
nodes.description: Tens of thousands of abandoned oil and gas wells in Alberta leaking methane. Companies went bankrupt, cleanup costs fall on taxpayers. $30-70 billion estimated liability.

nodes.title: Petro-State Identity Crisis
nodes.description: Alberta's economy and identity tied to oil. Carbon tax became culture war. Just Transition rhetoric alienating workers. No credible diversification plan at scale.

nodes.title: Trans Mountain Pipeline Controversy
nodes.description: Federal government bought and expanded pipeline for $34B+ (originally $4.5B estimate). Indigenous consent disputes. Climate vs economy framing.

nodes.title: Quebec Hydro Export Tension
nodes.description: Hydro-Québec exporting cheap clean power to US while domestic rates rise. New projects (La Romaine) face Indigenous opposition. Data centers and crypto miners consuming growing share.

nodes.title: Critical Minerals Rush
nodes.description: Canada positioning as alternative to China for lithium, nickel, cobalt. Ring of Fire in Northern Ontario stalled for decades by lack of infrastructure and Indigenous rights disputes.

nodes.title: Boil Water Advisories
nodes.description: Dozens of First Nations communities still under long-term drinking water advisories despite federal promises to resolve them. Some lasting 20+ years.

nodes.title: MMIWG Crisis Ongoing
nodes.description: Missing and Murdered Indigenous Women and Girls. National Inquiry completed 2019 with 231 Calls for Justice. Implementation slow. Systemic racism in policing persists.

nodes.title: Residential School Legacy
nodes.description: Intergenerational trauma from residential schools. Unmarked graves discoveries ongoing. TRC 94 Calls to Action — most unimplemented years later.

nodes.title: Duty to Consult Failures
nodes.description: Resource extraction projects routinely fail meaningful consultation with Indigenous communities. Consent treated as checkbox exercise. Legal battles dragging on for years.

nodes.title: Reserve Funding Model Broken
nodes.description: Federal funding for reserves chronically inadequate. Housing overcrowded and moldy. Schools underfunded compared to provincial equivalents. Indian Act paternalism persists.

nodes.title: TFW Program Abuse
nodes.description: Temporary Foreign Worker program used by Tim Hortons, farms, meat plants to suppress wages. Workers tied to single employer, vulnerable to exploitation. Pathway to PR unclear.

nodes.title: Gig Worker Misclassification
nodes.description: Uber, DoorDash, Skip, Instacart drivers classified as contractors. No benefits, no minimum wage guarantees, no sick days. Ontario Prop 22 equivalent attempts.

nodes.title: Construction Industry Death Rate
nodes.description: Construction is the deadliest industry in Canada. Pressure to cut corners on safety. Subcontractor chains diffuse accountability. WSIB/CNESST enforcement patchy.

nodes.title: Interprovincial Trade Barriers
nodes.description: Canada has internal trade barriers between provinces that are sometimes worse than international ones. Can't buy wine across provincial lines. Trades certifications don't transfer.

nodes.title: Brain Drain to US
nodes.description: Top Canadian tech, medical, academic talent leaving for US salaries 2-3x higher. TN visa makes it easy. Canada trains, US benefits.

nodes.title: Montreal Infrastructure Crumbling
nodes.description: Turcot, Champlain replacement took decades. Water main breaks daily. De la Concorde overpass collapsed in 2006 killing 5. Charbonneau Commission exposed systemic construction corruption.

nodes.title: REM Controversies
nodes.description: Montreal's new light rail built by CDPQ Infra (pension fund). Private profit from public transit. Limited stops, replaced bus lines, accessibility concerns. Exo integration poor.

nodes.title: Phoenix Pay System Disaster
nodes.description: Federal government payroll system that doesn't work since 2016. Over 100,000 public servants paid incorrectly. $2.5B+ spent trying to fix it. IBM project.

nodes.title: ArriveCAN Scandal
nodes.description: COVID border app that cost $60M+ for something that should have cost under $1M. Subcontractor chains, fake references. RCMP investigation.

nodes.title: Great Lakes Microplastics
nodes.description: Among the most microplastic-contaminated water bodies on earth. Drinking water source for 40M+ people. Industrial discharge, tire runoff, wastewater overflow.

nodes.title: BC Wildfire Escalation
nodes.description: 2023 broke all records — 2.8M hectares burned. Jasper partially destroyed 2024. Smoke blanketing cities for weeks. Insurance withdrawals starting. Fire seasons getting longer.

nodes.title: Atlantic Cod Collapse (Still)
nodes.description: 1992 moratorium still in effect. Stocks have not recovered in 30+ years. Ecosystem fundamentally altered. Community devastation in Newfoundland never reversed.

nodes.title: Permafrost Thaw Infrastructure
nodes.description: Northern Canadian buildings, roads, runways buckling as permafrost melts. Entire communities may need relocation. $50B+ in infrastructure at risk. Disproportionately affects Indigenous communities.

nodes.title: Salmon Decline (Pacific)
nodes.description: Pacific salmon runs at historic lows. Climate, fish farms, habitat loss, dams all contributing. First Nations food security and cultural practices threatened. Cohen Commission recommendations ignored.

nodes.title: Montreal Combined Sewer Overflows
nodes.description: Heavy rain causes raw sewage to dump directly into the St. Lawrence. Billions needed to separate storm and sanitary sewers. Happens dozens of times per year.

nodes.title: Agricultural Runoff (Lake Erie, St. Lawrence)
nodes.description: Phosphorus and nitrogen from Ontario/Quebec farms causing algal blooms. Blue-green algae making lakes unusable. Cyanotoxins in drinking water.

nodes.title: Big Five Bank Oligopoly
nodes.description: RBC, TD, BMO, Scotiabank, CIBC control Canadian banking. Record profits every quarter while raising fees. Mortgage market concentrated. Limited competition.

nodes.title: Mortgage Stress Test Loopholes
nodes.description: Designed to prevent over-borrowing but pushed people to alternative lenders with worse terms. Variable rate holders crushed by 2022-2023 rate hikes. Renewals hitting 2025-2026.

nodes.title: HELOC Debt Bomb
nodes.description: Canadians borrowed massively against home equity. ~$170B+ in HELOC debt. If housing prices correct, underwater borrowers with no savings.

nodes.title: Canadian Media Collapse
nodes.description: Postmedia owns most English newspapers and is gutting them. Bell Media laid off thousands. Local news disappearing. Bill C-18 backfired — Meta blocked Canadian news entirely.

nodes.title: Bill C-18 Blowback
nodes.description: Online News Act meant to force Meta/Google to pay for news. Meta removed all Canadian news from Facebook/Instagram instead. Google made deals. Net effect: less news distribution.

nodes.title: CBC Defunding Pressure
nodes.description: Conservative party promising to defund CBC. Public broadcaster already stretched thin. French-language Radio-Canada less politically targeted but also underfunded.

nodes.title: Migrant Farm Worker Conditions
nodes.description: SAWP workers (mostly Caribbean, Mexican) in Ontario/Quebec greenhouses and farms. Tied to employer, isolated, poor housing. Deaths from heat exposure. Limited healthcare access.

nodes.title: Canadian Farmland Foreign Ownership
nodes.description: Investment funds and foreign buyers purchasing Saskatchewan, Alberta, PEI farmland. Drives up land prices beyond what farming generates. Young farmers priced out.

nodes.title: Prairie Drought Intensification
nodes.description: Alberta and Saskatchewan experiencing more frequent severe droughts. Wheat, canola yields dropping. Cattle ranchers selling herds. Groundwater depleting.

nodes.title: Cargill Meat Monopoly
nodes.description: Two plants (Cargill, JBS) process 85%+ of Canadian beef. Single point of failure — COVID outbreak at Cargill High River plant shut down 40% of national capacity. Workers are TFWs.

nodes.title: VIA Rail Stagnation
nodes.description: Canada has no high-speed rail. VIA runs on CN/CP freight tracks, constantly delayed. Toronto-Montreal in 5+ hours. HFR project announced repeatedly, never built.

nodes.title: Canadian Airline Duopoly
nodes.description: Air Canada and WestJet dominate. Highest domestic airfares in G7. Budget carriers keep failing. Flying within Canada often costs more than flying to Europe.

nodes.title: Northern Supply Chain Fragility
nodes.description: Remote northern communities depend on ice roads (disappearing with warming) and seasonal barges. A gallon of milk can cost $15+ in Nunavut. Food insecurity is extreme.

nodes.title: Federal-Provincial Jurisdiction Gridlock
nodes.description: Healthcare, housing, environment all split between federal and provincial. Each blames the other. Carbon tax federalism fights. No coordination on immigration numbers vs services.

nodes.title: First-Past-the-Post (Still)
nodes.description: Trudeau promised electoral reform in 2015, abandoned it. FPTP produces false majorities. 39% of votes = 100% of power. Distorts representation, breeds cynicism.

nodes.title: Lobbying Registry Loopholes
nodes.description: Federal lobbying rules have huge gaps. Only 20%+ time spent lobbying requires registration. Former ministers become lobbyists after short cooling-off period.

nodes.title: SNC-Lavalin / Engineering Corruption
nodes.description: Pattern of corruption in Quebec engineering/construction firms (SNC-Lavalin, Dessau). Charbonneau Commission exposed collusion with politicians. DPA controversy reached PMO.

nodes.title: Canadian Tech Acquisition Problem
nodes.description: Promising Canadian startups (Shopify exception) routinely acquired by US companies. Nortel collapsed, BlackBerry collapsed. Innovation happens here, value captured elsewhere.

nodes.title: AI Talent Drain
nodes.description: Montreal is a global AI hub (Mila, Yoshua Bengio) but top researchers recruited away by US big tech. Google, Meta, Microsoft opened Montreal offices partly to siphon talent.

nodes.title: Canadian Mining Companies Abroad
nodes.description: TSX-listed mining firms responsible for environmental disasters and human rights abuses in Latin America, Africa. Barrick Gold in Papua New Guinea, Tanzania. Canada is a regulatory haven for mining firms.

nodes.title: Asbestos Export Legacy
nodes.description: Canada exported asbestos for decades, especially from Quebec (Thetford Mines). Blocked international bans. Town of Asbestos renamed to Val-des-Sources in 2020. Health consequences still unfolding globally.

nodes.title: Opioid Crisis (BC, Alberta, Ontario)
nodes.description: Toxic drug supply killing 20+ Canadians daily. BC declared public health emergency in 2016 — still ongoing. Safe supply debates. Supervised sites under political attack.

nodes.title: Encampment Wars
nodes.description: Homeless encampments in every major Canadian city. Cycles of clearing and re-establishment. Court rulings limiting evictions. No housing to send people to.

nodes.title: Immigration Volume vs Services Mismatch
nodes.description: Canada ramped to 500K+ permanent residents/year plus 1M+ temporary (students, TFWs). Housing, healthcare, schools not scaled to match. Backlash growing.

nodes.title: Quebec Language Tensions (Bill 96)
nodes.description: Strengthened French language law restricting English in business, government, education. Anglophones and allophones feeling pushed out. Healthcare exemptions narrow. Federal employees affected.

nodes.title: Child Welfare System (Indigenous Overrepresentation)
nodes.description: Indigenous children vastly overrepresented in foster care — more than during residential schools. Sixties Scoop patterns continuing. Jordan's Principle underfunded.

nodes.title: Arctic Sovereignty Under Threat
nodes.description: Northwest Passage becoming navigable as ice melts. Russia, China, US all asserting interests. Canada lacks military and infrastructure presence to back up claims.

nodes.title: Atmospheric River Events (BC)
nodes.description: 2021 atmospheric rivers caused catastrophic flooding in BC. Highways destroyed, towns cut off, 600K+ farm animals killed. $7.5B+ in damages. Infrastructure not built for this.

nodes.title: Lytton Heat Dome (2021)
nodes.description: Lytton BC hit 49.6°C — Canadian record and hotter than anywhere in recorded history for that latitude. Town burned down the next day. 619 heat-related deaths in BC that week.
